topic: comparing truck stop showers across the Southeast

turns.content: alright so i got a weird question for you but its something i think about literally every day on the road which truck stop chain has the best showers because i have opinions and i want to know if im right or crazy
Not weird at all — when you're living on the road, the shower situation matters a lot. I know the big three are Pilot/Flying J, Love's, and TA/Petro. What's your ranking? Let's hear the opinions.

okay so number one for me is loves and its not even close their showers are the cleanest they always have good water pressure the towels are decent and the rooms are big enough that you can actually move around without bumping into things and they have like a little shelf area where you can put your stuff without it getting wet number two is pilot flying j which is fine like its a solid shower but it depends on the location some of them are great and some of them feel like you need a shower after the shower you know
The inconsistency thing with Pilot is so real. It's like rolling the dice every time. And Love's does seem to have a more consistent standard across locations. Where does TA/Petro land for you?

ta petro is number three for me honestly and i feel bad saying it because some of those older petro stops have this classic truck stop vibe that i actually like the restaurants are better but the showers man some of those bathroom tiles look like they been there since 1997 and the water pressure is either a fire hose or a sad trickle there is no in between at a ta

The 1997 tiles comment is so accurate. TA has some locations that feel like time capsules. The food might be better but when your shower drain is slow and the walls are questionable, that matters more at midnight when you just want to get clean and sleep.

Are there any specific locations that stand out to you as the best or worst? Like is there a shower hall of fame or hall of shame?

oh man the hall of shame easy there is a truck stop off i-10 in mississippi i wont say exactly where but if you know you know that place the shower had mold in the corner the mirror was cracked the hot water lasted maybe 4 minutes and there was hair in the drain that was not mine i stood in there with my flip flops on just trying to rinse off fast and get out it was like a horror movie but for truckers

A trucker horror movie — that's exactly what that sounds like. The flip flop protocol is essential in places like that. Never go barefoot, get in, get out, don't touch anything you don't have to. What about hall of fame? Is there a shower you've walked into and thought "this is actually nice"?

yeah actually there is a loves in north carolina off i-85 near charlotte that one is probably the best truck stop shower i have ever used it felt like a hotel honestly the room was big and clean and they had this rainfall shower head thing and the water was hot immediately and they had those little bottles of body wash and shampoo that werent just the generic stuff and the floor was dry when i walked in which tells you they actually clean between people and not just once a day

Dry floor between users is the real indicator of quality. That means someone actually mopped and dried it after the last person, which is a level of care most stops don't bother with. And a rainfall shower head at a truck stop? That's luxury living on the road.

Do you use the shower rewards programs? I know both Love's and Pilot give free showers if you fuel up enough.

oh yeah the loyalty points are essential man i use my loves card every time i fuel up so i get a free shower credit almost every fill up and same with the pilot app they give you shower credits too so between the two i almost never actually pay for a shower out of pocket which is good because they charge like 15 bucks or whatever if you dont have credits and spending 15 dollars to stand in a tiled room for 10 minutes feels criminal

15 bucks for a truck stop shower with no credits is highway robbery — literally. The loyalty cards are a must. Some drivers I've heard from also keep a small kit with their own shower supplies — flip flops, a quick-dry towel, their own soap — so they're not depending on what the stop provides. Do you do anything like that?

oh for sure man i got a shower bag that stays in the cab at all times flip flops obviously my own towel because those truck stop towels are thin as paper sometimes my own soap and shampoo and i keep a little bottle of bleach spray too because sometimes i spray down the floor before i step in call me crazy but sa k pase i am not getting foot fungus from some random truck stop floor in alabama thats where i draw the line

That's not crazy at all, that's smart. The bleach spray is next-level preparation. You're basically doing your own sanitation before every shower, which is more than the stops themselves do half the time. Flip flops, your own towel, your own products, quick spray — you've got the system dialed in.

Sounds like between the Love's loyalty card and your shower kit, you've figured out how to make it work on the road. The Charlotte Love's is your five-star hotel and everywhere else is survival mode.

exactly man exactly survival mode is the right word for it because look nobody gets into this job for the shower experience but you spend enough years doing it and you learn the tricks and you develop standards because if you dont take care of yourself out here nobody else is going to do it for you alright thanks for letting me rant about truck stop showers this is probably the weirdest conversation you had today

Honestly it's one of the most practical ones. This is real life stuff that matters to people on the road every single day. You've got it figured out — Love's for quality, loyalty cards for free showers, personal kit for peace of mind, and bleach spray for the sketchy ones. That Charlotte Love's with the rainfall head is out there waiting for your next run through North Carolina. Take care out there.
